   <item>
      <title>Stephen Curry, Al Horford Lead Warriors To 126-121 Comeback Win Over Clippers</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285125/Stephen-Curry-Al-Horford-Lead-Warriors-To-126-121-Comeback-Win-Over-Clippers</link>
      <description><p>Stephen Curry scored 27 of his 35 points in the second half, Al Horford hit four 3-pointers during Golden State's electrifying fourth-quarter comeback, and the Warriors advanced in the NBA's play-in tournament with a 126-121 victory over the Los Angeles Clippers on Wednesday night.</p>
<p>Curry's seventh 3-pointer broke a tie with 50.4 seconds to play for the 10th-seeded Warriors, who erased a 13-point deficit in the fourth quarter.</p>
<p>Golden State finished on a 16-6 run and held Kawhi Leonard scoreless in the fourth until the final 16 seconds.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Thu, 16 Apr 2026 01:14:30 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285125/Stephen-Curry-Al-Horford-Lead-Warriors-To-126-121-Comeback-Win-Over-Clippers</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Curry_Stephen_gsw_191007.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>NBA Regular Season Viewership Hits 24-Year High At 170M U.S. Viewers</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285120/NBA-Regular-Season-Viewership-Hits-24-Year-High-At-170M-US-Viewers</link>
      <description><p>The NBA drew 170 million U.S. viewers during the 2025-26 regular season across its four primary broadcast platforms, which is its strongest viewership figures in 24 years and an 86 percent increase over the prior season.</p>
<p>The numbers mark Year 1 of the league's 11-year, $76 billion-plus media rights agreement signed in 2024, which added Amazon Prime Video and restored NBC/Peacock to the broadcast lineup alongside returning partners ABC/ESPN and NBA TV.</p>
<p>Average viewership across all four platforms reached its highest point in 13 years, climbing 35 percent over last season. A total of 57 telecasts drew average audiences of at least 2 million viewers, the most since 2011-12.</p>
<p>Fans logged more than 920 million total viewing hours, up 25% year-over-year and also the best figure since 2011-12.</p>
<p>The All-Star Game on NBC averaged 8.8 million viewers, the largest midseason showcase audience in 15 years. NBA Cup group stage games posted a 90 percent viewership gain.</p>
<p>Off the court, the league's social media platforms generated a record 228 billion views according to Videocites, a 13% rise over last season.</p>
<p>Arena attendance over the past three seasons has surpassed any comparable three-year span in league history, the NBA added.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Wed, 15 Apr 2026 15:50:50 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285120/NBA-Regular-Season-Viewership-Hits-24-Year-High-At-170M-US-Viewers</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Brown_Jaylen_bos_250504.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>LaMelo Ball Fined $35,000, Assessed Flagrant Foul 2 On Bam Adebayo Play</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285122/LaMelo-Ball-Fined-$35000-Assessed-Flagrant-Foul-2-On-Bam-Adebayo-Play</link>
      <description><p>LaMelo Ball has been fined $35,000 and assessed a Flagrant Foul 2 upon league office review for making unnecessary and reckless contact with Miami Heat center-forward Bam Adebayo that created a significant injury risk.</p>
<p>The incident occurred with 11:13 remaining in the second quarter of the Charlotte Hornets' 127-126 overtime win over the Heat on April 14 in the NBA Play-In Tournament.</p>
<p>Ball has been fined an additional $25,000 for using profane language during a live postgame television interview.</p>
<p>Ball will not be suspended for Friday's Play-In game. </p></description>
      <pubDate>Wed, 15 Apr 2026 22:00:40 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285122/LaMelo-Ball-Fined-$35000-Assessed-Flagrant-Foul-2-On-Bam-Adebayo-Play</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Ball_Lamelo_cha_201215.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>A'ja Wilson, Aces Sign WNBA Record Three-Year, $5M Supermax Contract</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285117/Aja-Wilson-Aces-Sign-WNBA-Record-Three-Year-$5M-Supermax-Contract</link>
      <description><p>A'ja Wilson has agreed to sign a three-year, $5 million supermax contract with the Las Vegas Aces. The deal is the largest in WNBA history and was negotiated by Jade-Li English of Klutch Sports Group.</p>
<p>Wilson is a three-time WNBA champion and four-time WNBA Most Valuable Player.</p>
<p>Wilson was the first overall pick in the 2018 WNBA draft out of South Carolina after also winning a NCAA championship in college.</p>
<p><a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/player/Aja-Wilson/Summary/3001159">During her MVP season in 2025</a>, Wilson averaged 23.4 points, 10.2 rebounds, 3.0 assists, 2.3 blocks and 1.6 steals with a PER of 33.3. <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/wnba/stats/2025/Advanced_Stats/Qualified/points/All/desc/1/Regular_Season">Wilson's WNBA leading PER was well ahead of second ranked Napheesa Collier at 29.8</a>.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Wed, 15 Apr 2026 14:36:51 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285117/Aja-Wilson-Aces-Sign-WNBA-Record-Three-Year-$5M-Supermax-Contract</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Wilson_Aja_lva_240522.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Sean Sweeney Considered NBA's Leading First-Time Head Coach Candidate</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285119/Sean-Sweeney-Considered-NBAs-Leading-First-Time-Head-Coach-Candidate</link>
      <description><p>Sean Sweeney is expected to be one of the leading head coaching candidates during the upcoming offseason cycle. Sweeney joined the San Antonio Spurs as associate head coach under Mitch Johnson last year after four seasons as the lead assistant for Jason Kidd with the Dallas Mavericks. </p>
<p>"He's the top available (first-time NBA head coach) by a wide margin," one Eastern Conference scout told The Athletic.</p>
<p>Sweeney was brought up to The Athletic the most amongst insiders when inquiring about potential first-time coaches.</p>
<p>Before joining the Mavericks, Sweeney was an assistant coach under Kidd during the early part of Giannis Antetokounmpo's career where he was the future two-time MVP's primary player development coach. </p>
<p>Sweeney then worked under Dwane Casey with the Detroit Pistons. </p>
<p>The Milwaukee Bucks and New Orleans Pelicans are the two teams currently with head coaching vacancies.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Wed, 15 Apr 2026 15:45:27 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285119/Sean-Sweeney-Considered-NBAs-Leading-First-Time-Head-Coach-Candidate</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/nba_sanantoniospurs.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Russell Westbrook Will Return To Kings If Wanted Back</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285115/Russell-Westbrook-Will-Return-To-Kings-If-Wanted-Back</link>
      <description><p>Russell Westbrook's season with the Sacramento Kings ended with the team tied for last place in the Western Conference and Westbrook sidelined due to injury. Despite the downer ending to the year, Westbrook would like to return to Sacramento next season.</p>
<p>"If I'm welcomed back, then I'll be back," Westbrook said when asked if he'd return to the Kings for another season.</p>
<p>The veteran point guard was signed shortly before the start of the regular season. Westbrook went on to average 15.2 points, 5.4 rebounds, 6.7 assists and 1.3 steals in 64 games.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Wed, 15 Apr 2026 09:57:31 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285115/Russell-Westbrook-Will-Return-To-Kings-If-Wanted-Back</guid>

   <item>
      <title>Deni Avdija's 41 Points Leads Blazers Back To Playoffs For First Time Since 2021</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285116/Deni-Avdijas-41-Points-Leads-Blazers-Back-To-Playoffs-For-First-Time-Since-2021</link>
      <description><p>Deni Avdija scored 41 points to go with 12 assists and seven rebounds in the Portland Trail Blazers' 114-110 win at the Phoenix Suns to clinch the No. 7 seed in the Western Conference Playoffs. <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/nba/teams/Portland-Trail-Blazers/24/playoff-history">The Blazers hadn't reached the playoffs since 2021</a> when they were eliminated in the first round by the Denver Nuggets in six games.</p>
<p>Avdija struggled in the second half of the regular season, but he showcased why he was an All-Star for the first time in his career with a playoff spot on the line.</p>
<p>"It's the best thing that's ever happened to me in my career so far," Avdija said.</p>
<p>Portland's outstanding perimeter defenders limited Devin Booker to just 17 field goal attempts as he finished with 22 points. Jalen Green scored 35 points on 14-for-29 from the floor.</p>
<p><a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/nba/boxscore/2026-04-14/Portland-at-Phoenix/522371">The Blazers scored 1.194 points per possession compared to 1.152 for the Suns</a>.</p>
<p>The Blazers will play the San Antonio Spurs in the first round as Victor Wembanyama makes his playoff debut. <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/betting/basketball/nba/playoffs/odds/">The Spurs are heavy favorites in the series at -2500</a>.</p>
<p>The Suns will host the winner of Wednesday's game between the Los Angeles Clippers and Golden State Warriors. <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/betting/news/clippers-warriors-playin-prediction-04-15-2026/">The Clippers are -208 favorites in the game</a>.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Wed, 15 Apr 2026 11:46:17 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285116/Deni-Avdijas-41-Points-Leads-Blazers-Back-To-Playoffs-For-First-Time-Since-2021</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Avdija_Deni_por_241028.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>NBA Investigating LaMelo Ball For Tripping Bam Adebayo In Play-In</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285121/NBA-Investigating-LaMelo-Ball-For-Tripping-Bam-Adebayo-In-Play-In</link>
      <description><p>LaMelo Ball is under investigation by the NBA for tripping Miami Heat center Bam Adebayo during Tuesday's play-in game, which the Charlotte Hornets won 127-126 in overtime.</p>
<p>The league has contacted both franchises and will interview Ball, sources said. The incident is under review for a possible flagrant foul 1 or flagrant foul 2 upgrade, though sources indicated a suspension is unlikely.</p>
<p>Adebayo suffered a lower back injury on the play and did not return, finishing with just 11 minutes of action. Ball scored the winning layup with 4.7 seconds remaining.</p>
<p>The play occurred early in the second quarter. After Ball's shot was blocked, Adebayo grabbed the rebound near the sideline. Ball, falling to the floor, swiped at Adebayo's foot, sending him hard to the ground with no whistle. Officials could not review the play because it was not stopped immediately.</p>
<p>"The play wasn't whistled in real time," referee Zach Zarba told a pool reporter. "Play continued with a fast break. And because play wasn't stopped immediately, and there was no whistle on the play, the window to review the play was closed."</p>
<p>Erik Spoelstra called for Ball's ejection following the loss.</p>
<p>"I don't think it's cute," Spoelstra said. "I don't think it's funny. I think it's a stupid play. It's a dangerous play. Obviously, our best player was out. I'm not making an excuse. The Hornets played great and they made those plays down the stretch. We had our opportunities to win. That's a shame. You should be penalized for that. I don't think that belongs in the game. Tripping guys, shenanigans."</p>
<p>Ball issued an apology after the game.</p>
<p>"I apologize on that one," Ball said. "I got hit in the head [on the play] and didn't really know where I was. But I'm going to check on him, see if he's OK and everything."</p>
<p>Following the game, video surfaced of a January 2024 regular-season encounter in which Ball appeared to deliberately reach for Adebayo's leg while on the floor.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Wed, 15 Apr 2026 19:52:49 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285121/NBA-Investigating-LaMelo-Ball-For-Tripping-Bam-Adebayo-In-Play-In</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Ball_Lamelo_cha_201215.jpg" />
   </item>

   <item>
      <title>LaMelo Ball Scores 30 Points In Hornets' Win Over Heat In Play-In Game</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285111/LaMelo-Ball-Scores-30-Points-In-Hornets-Win-Over-Heat-In-Play-In-Game</link>
      <description><p>The Charlotte Hornets defeated the Miami Heat 127-126 in the play-in game on Tuesday night, eliminating the Heat from the playoffs.</p>
<p>LaMelo Ball had 30 points and 10 assists in the win, with Miles Bridges adding 28 points and nine rebounds.</p>
<p>Ball is now the fourth player to have at least 30 points and 10 assists in a play-in game.</p>
<p>Davion Mitchell finished with 28 points for the Heat. Andrew Wiggins added 27 points in the loss.</p>
<p>Bam Adebayo left the game with a lower back injury in the second quarter after he was tripped by Ball.</p>
<p>The Hornets are set to face the loser of the matchup between the Sixers and Magic on Friday.</p>
<p>The Heat will miss the playoffs for the first time since the 2018-19 season.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Wed, 15 Apr 2026 01:17:24 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285111/LaMelo-Ball-Scores-30-Points-In-Hornets-Win-Over-Heat-In-Play-In-Game</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Ball_Lamelo_cha_201215.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Erik Spoelstra Says LaMelo Ball 'Should Have Been Thrown Out' For Tripping Bam Adebayo</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285112/Erik-Spoelstra-Says-LaMelo-Ball-Should-Have-Been-Thrown-Out-For-Tripping-Bam-Adebayo</link>
      <description><p>Head coach Erik Spoelstra said that LaMelo Ball should have been ejected from the <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285111/LaMelo-Ball-Scores-30-Points-In-Hornets-Win-Over-Heat-In-Play-In-Game">Miami Heat's 127-126 loss</a> to the Charlotte Hornets in the play-in game after tripping Bam Adebayo in the second quarter.</p>
<p>"I didn't see it [when it happened], but I don't think it's cute," Spoelstra said after the loss. "I don't think it's funny. I think it's a stupid play. It's a dangerous play. Obviously, our best player was out.</p>
<p>"I'm not making an excuse. The Hornets played great and they made those plays down the stretch. We had our opportunities to win. That's a shame. You should be penalized for that. I don't think that belongs in the game -- tripping guys, shenanigans."</p>
<p>Adebayo was ruled out for the game due to a lower back injury. The center had six points and three rebounds when he was injured.</p>
<p>Spoelstra then put blame on the refs for not seeing the trip.</p>
<p>"Somebody has got to see that and that he should have been thrown out of the game for that," added Spoelstra. "I don't know him from anyone. There's no place in the game for that."</p>
<p>Ball, who finished with 30 points and 10 assists, apologized for the trip after the game.</p>
<p>"I apologize on that one," Ball said. "I got hit in the head [on the play] and didn't really know where I was. But I'm going to check on him, see if he's OK and everything."</p>
<p>With the loss, the Heat missed the playoffs for the first time since the 2018-19 season.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Wed, 15 Apr 2026 01:26:48 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285112/Erik-Spoelstra-Says-LaMelo-Ball-Should-Have-Been-Thrown-Out-For-Tripping-Bam-Adebayo</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Adebayo_Bam_mia_220725.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Luka Doncic, Austin Reaves Out Indefinitely; Lakers Won't Give Update This Week</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285110/Luka-Doncic-Austin-Reaves-Out-Indefinitely;-Lakers-Wont-Give-Update-This-Week</link>
      <description><p>Los Angeles Lakers coach JJ Redick confirmed Tuesday that Luka Doncic and Austin Reaves remain out indefinitely as the team begins preparation for its first-round playoff series against the Houston Rockets, leaving the club without its starting backcourt for at least the early stages of the postseason.</p>
<p>"They're out indefinitely. I'm not going to have an update for you this week," Redick said, repeating the message for emphasis.</p>
<p>Doncic, who suffered a Grade 2 hamstring strain on April 2, has been in Madrid undergoing treatment at Real Madrid's facilities, where members of his medical team are based. A league source confirmed he is set to fly back to Los Angeles on Friday to rejoin the team ahead of Game 1, though he will not play. Grade 2 hamstring strains typically carry a four-to-six week recovery timeline.</p>
<p>Reaves sustained a Grade 2 oblique strain in the same game and has been receiving treatment in Los Angeles. He attended Tuesday's practice but did not participate. His injury carries an identical four-to-six week timetable.</p>
<p>With both guards sidelined, Redick said the organization's focus is on extending the series long enough for the two to potentially return later in the playoffs. Redick indicated the burden of playmaking will not fall exclusively on 41-year-old LeBron James.</p>
<p>"That's what we've had to do for 82 games for the last two years, so it's not any different," Redick said. "We're mindful. I do think we have other options other than just him having the ball and going against a set defense every single possession."</p>
<p>Redick said the Lakers distilled their series preparation against Houston to two priorities.</p>
<p>"Take care of the ball and box out," he said.</p>
<p><a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/betting/news/">The Lakers enter the series as +550 underdogs</a>, according to oddsmakers.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Tue, 14 Apr 2026 21:08:15 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285110/Luka-Doncic-Austin-Reaves-Out-Indefinitely;-Lakers-Wont-Give-Update-This-Week</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Doncic_Luka_lal_250327.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>2026 NBA Draft Set For Two Nights Beginning Tuesday June 23</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285109/2026-NBA-Draft-Set-For-Two-Nights-Beginning-Tuesday-June-23</link>
      <description><p>The NBA has scheduled the <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/nba/draft/draft_simulator">2026 NBA draft</a> for Tuesday, June 23rd and Wednesday June 24th. Both nights will be televised by ESPN and begin at 8 p.m. ET.</p>
<p>The <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/nba/draft/lottery_simulator">NBA draft lottery</a> is scheduled for Sunday, May 10th. The Washington Wizards, Indiana Pacers and Brooklyn Nets each share a 14 percent chance of landing the number one overall pick and a 52.1 percent chance of a top-4 pick. The Utah Jazz are close behind in terms of the odds at 48.1 percent in the top-4 and 12.5 percent at No. 1. </p>
<p><a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285013/AJ-Dybantsa-Moves-To-No-1-In-ESPNs-2026-NBA-Draft-Big-Board-Top-Of-Class-Remains-Open">A.J. Dybantsa, Darryn Peterson, Cameron Boozer and Caleb Wilson are expected to be the first four picks in some order</a>. There are several highly touted guards right behind them in Keaton Wagler, Darius Acufff Jr., Kingston Flemings and Mikel Brown Jr.</p>
<p>The NBA draft has historically been held on a Thursday, but they switched to a Wednesday and Thursday format when they began to hold the first and second round on separate nights. The NBA is believed to have moved up the 2026 draft to a Tuesday and Wednesday in order to avoid competing against the United States' final World Cup group stage match against T&uuml;rkiye on Thursday, June 25th in primetime. </p></description>
      <pubDate>Tue, 14 Apr 2026 16:11:21 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285109/2026-NBA-Draft-Set-For-Two-Nights-Beginning-Tuesday-June-23</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Dybantsa_AJ_byu_251212.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Pelicans Have No Intention Of Trading Zion Williamson In Offseason</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285108/Pelicans-Have-No-Intention-Of-Trading-Zion-Williamson-In-Offseason</link>
      <description><p>The future of Zion Williamson with the New Orleans Pelicans has been an open question for multiple offseasons dating back to the 2023 draft when he was linked with a trade for the draft rights to Scoot Henderson. But Joe Dumars continues to signal that Williamson will remain with the Pelicans.</p>
<p>"We have no intentions of doing that," said Dumars on Tuesday when asked about a potential trade of Williamson. "We look forward to working with him next year."</p>
<p>Williamson played in 62 games for the Pelicans, averaging 21.0 points, 5.7 rebounds and 3.2 assists in 29.7 minutes. Williamson played in just 30 games in 2024-25, but he played in 70 during the 2023-24 season.</p>
<p>Williamson, who is represented by <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/info/agent_clients/Bill-Duffy/16">Bill Duffy</a>, cannot become a free agent until 2028.</p>
<p>For his part, Williamson has also spoken publicly about his desire to remain with the Pelicans.</p></description>

   <item>
      <title>Stephen Curry, Kristaps Porzingis, Al Horford On Minutes Restriction In Play-In Game</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285103/Stephen-Curry-Kristaps-Porzingis-Al-Horford-On-Minutes-Restriction-In-Play-In-Game</link>
      <description><p>Stephen Curry, Kristaps Porzingis and Al Horford will play under minutes restrictions Wednesday when the Golden State Warriors faces the Los Angeles Clippers in a play-in game, head coach Steve Kerr confirmed.</p>
<p>All three will be held under 40 minutes in the game despite the reality that a Warriors loss ends their season.</p>
<p>Golden State secured the final play-in spot at 37-45 and is seeking a rematch after falling to the Los Angeles Clippers 115-110 in Sunday's regular-season finale.</p>
<p>Curry has battled knee problems throughout the season. Horford has been dealing with a calf issue, while Porzingis and Draymond Green have both been managing sore backs. Jimmy Butler, meanwhile, has been sidelined since tearing his ACL in early January.</p>
<p>Kerr has been vocal about the NBA's schedule contributing to the league's injury concerns.</p>
<p>"I know this will not be a popular opinion in the league office, but I will continue to say it because it's obvious we need to play fewer games - we need to take 10 games off the schedule," said Kerr in March. "I think it would be a more competitive and healthier league if we played fewer games."</p>
<p>For Los Angeles, Kawhi Leonard sat out the final regular-season games to prepare for the play-in. Leonard posted a career-high 27.9 points, 6.4 rebounds and 3.6 assists this season, appearing in 68 games after injury limited him to 37 the prior year.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Tue, 14 Apr 2026 10:41:28 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285103/Stephen-Curry-Kristaps-Porzingis-Al-Horford-On-Minutes-Restriction-In-Play-In-Game</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Curry_Stephen_gsw_250430.jpg" />
   </item>

      <title>Tyrese Haliburton Dealing With Shingles</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285102/Tyrese-Haliburton-Dealing-With-Shingles</link>
      <description><p>Indiana Pacers guard Tyrese Haliburton was feeling good with his return from the torn Achilles' tendon he suffered in last year's NBA Finals. Unfortunately, Haliburton is now battling a case of shingles, which has caused him pain, itching and to gain weight.</p>
<p>"I couldn't even go in front of a camera if I wanted to early, because my eye was basically closed shut," Haliburton said. "It was all over my face."</p>
<p>The Pacers star said that the medication he's used to deal with the symptoms has caused him to gain weight. Haliburton also detailed other impacts he's dealt with as a result of the illness.</p>
<p>"I've lost part of my eyebrow; my eye is always swollen from itching it," Haliburton said. "I have good days and bad days, but for the most part it's bad days. So, it has not been any fun."</p></description>

      <title>Michael Porter Jr Hopes To Extend With Nets</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285101/Michael-Porter-Jr-Hopes-To-Extend-With-Nets</link>
      <description><p>The Brooklyn Nets are rebuilding, but if Michael Porter Jr. has his way, he'll be a part of whatever comes next. Porter is entering the final year of his contract, and would like to extend or re-sign with the Nets.</p>
<p>"If it was up to me, I'd love to sign an extension with this franchise," said Porter. "I feel like we mesh. We have great vibes, great energy in the locker room, and to be a part of something that's building in a positive way, a positive momentum, that'd be awesome. I'd love to spend many years in Brooklyn, make this my home and build and watch this franchise take off, because we saw glimpses of it this year.</p>
<p>"There was a lot of positives. Front office-wise, they have the ability to make some moves and make us even better. If the goal is to win, which we all know it is, then I'd love to make this my home, and that'd definitely be a cool thing for sure."</p>
<p>Porter also touched on things that he knows he needs to improve, noting he didn't finish the season as strongly as he had started it.</p>
<p>"Once All-Star break happened and I realized I wasn't an All-Star, I had a little slippage in my focus, in my preparation," said Porter. "That can't happen regardless of what's going on with the team and the record."</p></description>

   <item>
      <title>Steve Kerr Wants To Return Next Season Despite Unresolved Contract</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285100/Steve-Kerr-Wants-To-Return-Next-Season-Despite-Unresolved-Contract</link>
      <description><p>Steve Kerr wants to remain the Golden State Warriors' head coach, but his future with the franchise is unresolved as his contract expires at the end of this season. The 60-year-old signed a two-year, $35 million extension in 2024 covering only through the current campaign.</p>
<p>Kerr and general manager Mike Dunleavy have maintained a consistent position throughout the season that all future decisions will be addressed once play concludes. A team source recently confirmed that nothing has changed and that all parties plan to communicate openly when the time comes. The Warriors will play at the Los Angeles Clippers this week in the 9/10 play-in game.</p>
<p>"It doesn't factor in," Kerr said when asked whether the season's outcome would influence his decision. "That's something I'll address after the season ends. My focus is all on the Clippers and I'm excited about trying to get in."</p>
<p>There has been no indication of a split between Kerr and the organization. His desire to stay is rooted in his relationship with Stephen Curry and the culture he has built in Golden State since taking the job in 2014.</p>
<p>The season that tested that culture was unlike any Kerr has navigated in 12 years on the Warriors' bench. Jimmy Butler tore his ACL in January. Moses Moody suffered a patellar tendon rupture in March. Curry missed more than two months with a knee injury. Jonathan Kuminga was traded ahead of the February deadline after a prolonged standoff. A public dispute with Draymond Green surfaced just before Christmas, prompting a later apology from Kerr.</p>
<p>Despite the turbulence, those who have watched Kerr this season describe a coach still deeply engaged in the process.</p>
<p>"Coming in and seeing the guys and having a film session, trying to help them win," said Kerr when asked what motivates him. "And having dinner with the coaches, and the camaraderie that exists when you are in the foxhole together. You've lost five in a row, and it sucks - it's all relative. It sucks, but it's so awesome to be with a group of great guys and try to win a basketball game. I love all of it. And you have to embrace the challenge and the fact that every year's just gonna be different."</p>
<p>Kerr has won nine NBA championships as a player and coach and added an Olympic gold medal coaching Team USA in Paris in 2024.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Tue, 14 Apr 2026 07:07:54 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285100/Steve-Kerr-Wants-To-Return-Next-Season-Despite-Unresolved-Contract</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Kerr_Steve_gsw_240113.jpg" />
   </item>

      <title>Anthony Davis Wants Championship Plan From Wizards Before Committing Long-Term</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285096/Anthony-Davis-Wants-Championship-Plan-From-Wizards-Before-Committing-Long-Term</link>
      <description><p>Anthony Davis made clear Monday that the Washington Wizards must present a credible path to contention this summer, setting up what figures to be one of the NBA offseason's most consequential front office conversations. Davis said he plans to meet with Monumental Basketball president Michael Winger and general manager Will Dawkins in the coming months to evaluate the franchise's competitive timeline.</p>
<p>Davis stopped well short of requesting a trade, acknowledging his contractual obligations while leaving the door open depending on what those discussions produce.</p>
<p>"Whether we figure something out in the summer (that's) good or bad, whatever ... if it doesn't get figured out, then come October, mid-October, late October, I will have a Washington Wizards uniform on, and I will go out there, and I will do everything I can to win a basketball game," Davis said. "In the summer, right now, I'm going to compete, train and have the mindset that I will be here next year. Not only because I'm under contract, but also, I like these young guys. I ain't going to lie. I feel like they're all my children."</p>
<p>Davis was direct about the difficulty of the Wizards' situation, expressing doubt that Washington can leap from the league's worst record in 2025-26 to a championship contender in one offseason, even with himself and Trae Young on the roster.</p>
<p>"I've been in this league a long time, and I've been with losing teams, been on losing teams," Davis said. "It's very hard to be a losing team and then a championship contender, right? I think one team has only done it: Boston in '08. Even when I was in New Orleans and we had two losing seasons, then we made the playoffs our third year - that, within itself, is a huge jump. What is our plan for winning going into next season? And how? Not like this is a plan, but how do we execute that? And based on that, we'll kind of see what happens."</p>
<p>Davis' contract adds another layer of complexity. He is owed $58.5 million in 2026-27 and holds a $62.8 million player option for 2027-28. Industry speculation suggests the 33-year-old may seek additional years on his current deal, though whether Washington would entertain that given his injury history and the organization's focus on its young core remains unclear.</p>
<p>Monday marked Davis' first formal availability with Washington-area media since his surprise trade from the Dallas Mavericks in early February. Despite a rocky initial perception of the franchise, Davis said his concerns faded quickly after arriving.</p>
<p>"I can personally say now it's not what people think or what people make it seem," Davis said. "Yes, the losing is part of it, so people kind of tie that with the organization. But the organization within itself is totally different from what people think they're seeing."</p></description>
      <pubDate>Mon, 13 Apr 2026 22:59:21 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285096/Anthony-Davis-Wants-Championship-Plan-From-Wizards-Before-Committing-Long-Term</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Davis_Anthony_dal_260109.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Azzi Fudd Selected No. 1 In WNBA Draft By Dallas Wings</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285095/Azzi-Fudd-Selected-No-1-In-WNBA-Draft-By-Dallas-Wings</link>
      <description><p>Former UConn star Azzi Fudd was selected first overall by the Dallas Wings in the 2026 WNBA Draft, earning a $500,000 salary under the league's newly ratified collective bargaining agreement, nearly seven times what teammate Paige Bueckers earned as the top pick in 2025.</p>
<p>"I don't think it's fully sunk in. It's nothing I could have imagined," Fudd said. "Such a surreal feeling."</p>
<p>Fudd joins Bueckers in Dallas, reuniting the former Huskies teammates and giving UConn a record seven No. 1 overall selections in WNBA Draft history. Bueckers was present at the draft alongside several of Fudd's former Connecticut teammates.</p>
<p>"Paige is an incredible player, everyone knows that," Fudd said. "She's someone that makes playing basketball with easy."</p>
<p>Fudd averaged career highs across multiple categories in 2025-26, posting 17.3 points, 48 percent shooting, 45 percent from three, and 3.1 assists per game. She made 117 three-pointers this past season, fourth most in UConn history, and 196 over the past two seasons, the most in Division I over that span. Her path to Dallas included significant injury hurdles, including a torn ACL that limited her to just two games in 2023-24.</p>
<p>UCLA made history as the draft's deepest program. Lauren Betts went fourth to Washington, Gabriela Jaquez fifth to Chicago, and Kiki Rice sixth to expansion franchise Toronto, breaking UConn's record of four first-round selections set in 2002. The Bruins tied the all-time record of five total players drafted, shared previously by Tennessee, Notre Dame, and South Carolina.</p>
<p>Minnesota took TCU guard Olivia Miles with the No. 2 pick. Seattle selected Spain center Awa Fam Thiam third before Portland, in its first-ever pick as an expansion franchise, chose Spanish guard Iyana Martin Carrion.</p>
<p>Under the new CBA, second and third-round picks will earn $270,000, a figure that surpasses the previous maximum salary under the old agreement.</p>
<p>"I'm just blessed and grateful to come at this time," said No. 8 pick Flau'jae Johnson, who was traded to Seattle after being selected by Golden State. "My goal is to leave it better than I found it."</p></description>
      <pubDate>Mon, 13 Apr 2026 21:03:29 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285095/Azzi-Fudd-Selected-No-1-In-WNBA-Draft-By-Dallas-Wings</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Fudd_Azzi_uconn_260404.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Top-5 Net Rating Teams Each Also Had Top-10 Offensive, Defensive Efficiency</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285094/Top-5-Net-Rating-Teams-Each-Also-Had-Top-10-Offensive-Defensive-Efficiency</link>
      <description><p>Five NBA teams finished the 2025-26 regular season ranked in the top 10 in both offensive and defensive efficiency, a threshold that historically identifies legitimate championship contenders. Since 1974, roughly 75 percent of all NBA champions posted top-10 marks in both categories during the regular season before going on to win the NBA championship.</p>
<p>The Oklahoma City Thunder led the group on the defensive end, ranking first in defensive rating at 107.2, while posting a 119.2 offensive rating, good for seventh in the league. The San Antonio Spurs finished third defensively at 110.9 and fifth offensively at 119.3. The Detroit Pistons ranked second in defensive efficiency at 109.7 paired with a 10th-place offensive rating of 117.7.</p>
<p>The Boston Celtics posted the second-best offensive rating in the league at 120.7 and ranked fourth defensively at 112.8. The New York Knicks rounded out the group, finishing third offensively at 120.0 and eighth defensively at 113.4.</p>
<p>All five clubs also finished in the top five in <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/nba/team-stats/2026/Advanced_Stats/Team_Totals/Regular_Season/ediff/desc">overall net rating</a>, underlining the two-way consistency of the NBA's leading teams this season.</p>
<p>The Denver Nuggets and Cleveland Cavaliers have better <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/betting/news/nba-championship-betting-odds/">NBA championship odds entering the playoffs</a> than the Knicks and Pistons despite the statistical profile.</p>
<p>Those falling outside the top 10 in one category have typically compensated with a top-3 ranking in the other to remain viable title contenders. Teams outside both thresholds have rarely, if ever, won a championship in the modern era. The Houston Rockets in 1995 are the only team that finished the regular season outside the top-10 in both categories as a true historical outlier. </p></description>
      <pubDate>Mon, 13 Apr 2026 21:01:17 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285094/Top-5-Net-Rating-Teams-Each-Also-Had-Top-10-Offensive-Defensive-Efficiency</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Mazzulla_Joe_bos_240606.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Luka Doncic Returning To Los Angeles From Spain Before Start Of Playoffs</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285093/Luka-Doncic-Returning-To-Los-Angeles-From-Spain-Before-Start-Of-Playoffs</link>
      <description><p>Luka Doncic is returning to Los Angeles on Friday after spending the past week in Spain receiving multiple treatments on his left hamstring strain. Doncic is rehabilitating a Grade 2 left hamstring strain.</p>
<p>The Los Angeles Lakers haven't provided a timetable for when Doncic may be able to return from his injury. The Lakers open their first round <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/betting/basketball/nba/playoffs/odds/">playoff series</a> on Saturday against the Houston Rockets.</p>
<p>Grade 2 hamstring strains typically require a month recovery. Doncic missed part of the first round of the playoffs in 2022 with the Dallas Mavericks with a hamstring injury. Doncic eventually returned and helped lead the Mavericks to the Western Conference Finals.</p>
<p>The Lakers are also without Austin Reaves, who is dealing with an oblique injury.</p>
<p>Doncic is also petitioning the NBA to grant him regular season award eligibility after appearing in 64 games, one short of the minimum. Doncic missed two games in December for the birth of his daughter in Slovenia. </p></description>
